title: OpenClaw movePathToTrash resolved 'trash' executable via workspace-influenced service PATH
description: In src/plugin-sdk/browser-maintenance.ts the movePathToTrash maintenance helper invoked runExec("trash", [targetPath], ...) using only the bare command name "trash", relying on PATH resolution to locate the executable. The service PATH is assembled (in src/daemon/service-env.ts) partly from env-configured bin directories such as PNPM_HOME, NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX, BUN_INSTALL, VOLTA_HOME, NVM_DIR, FNM_DIR and NIX_PROFILES, which can be derived from a lower-trust workspace. A workspace-derived PATH entry could therefore point at an attacker-controlled directory containing a malicious 'trash' binary, causing the daemon to execute an unintended local executable during cleanup/maintenance (untrusted search path, CWE-426).
